Notice Of Motion And Motion To Adopt Proposed Order Detailing Operation Of Equity Receivership Over Judgment Debtor Eprazel, Inc.
Off Calendar Nov-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs KEVIN LAHR,and STEPHEN THOMPSON's Motion To Adopt Proposed Order Detailing Operation Of Equity Receivership Over Judgment Debtor Eprazel, Inc.

Plaintiff and Judgment Creditors Kevin Lahr and Stephen Thompson's Motion To Adopt Proposed Order Detailing Operation Of Equity Receivership Over Judgment Debtor Eprazel, Inc. is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART.

Good cause appearing the court will execute the proposed order that is Exhibit A to the Javeri-Weeks declaration, except that the court will strike out paragraph 3(k). This authority or protection was not conferred upon the receiver's appointment. The court does not find good cause to adopt the provision now. The court understands the receiver has quasi-judicial immunity for good faith actions within the scope of their authority. The provision appears to provide expanded immunity for, for example, gross negligence, intentional misconduct, or actions beyond the scope of their authority. Such immunity would be contrary to public policy.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Off Calendar Apr-04-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF KEVIN LAHR, STEPHEN THOMPSON's Motion To File Under Seal.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiffs' "motion to file under seal" is denied without prejudice. The motion does not meet the requirements of CRC 2.550 et seq.; e.g., the proposed redactions are not "narrowly tailored."

If plaintiffs pursue sealing with a new motion, they shall lodge a table for the court's in camera review that (a) states the full text of each proposed redaction; (b) provides plaintiffs' ground(s) for each redaction, complete with legal authority and (c) in like fashion, provides defendant's argument(s), if any. (See H.B. Fuller Co. v. Doe (2007) 151 Cal.App.4th 879, 894.)

Judgment Creditors Notice Of Motion For A Turnover Order And Related Relief; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Nov-03-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFRENCE):
Atty. JESSICA TARAN, Esq. of Taran Law (Tel: 917-686-0195) for plaintiffs;
Atty. JAMIE C. COUCHE, Esq. of Anderson, Poole & Couche, P.C. (Tel: 415-956-6413) for defendant Eprazel, Inc.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS KEVIN LAHR and STEPHEN THOMPSON's Motion For A Turnover Order And Related Relief.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiffs' motion for turnover of defendants' intellectual property to San Francisco's sheriff under CCP 699.040 is denied.

Plaintiffs concede "there is virtually no caselaw discussing a Section 699.040 turnover of intellectual property." (Memo. 5:4-5.) This for good reasons: among them, no showing is made that the sheriff has the capability to take the IP "into custody" much less the expertise to conduct a sale of it. (See CCP 699.040(a)(1).)

The one arguably relevant case plaintiffs' memo cites applies Washington state law and regards copyrights - a readily saleable traditional form of IP nothing like the trade secrets, software code, "user stories" and "requirements documents" at issue here. (Memo. 2:1-6, 8:13-15.) In contrast, defendants' cited cases deny turnover of intangible property that cannot be taken into physical custody. (Palacio Del Mar Homeowners Assn. Inc. v. McMahon (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 1386, 1391; Pacific Decision Sciences Corp. v. Sup. Ct. (2004) 121 Cal.App.4th 1100, 1109.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
